title: `INSERT SELECT` with implicit `GROUP BY` by destination partition key
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Use case
There's "Too many partitions for single INSERT block" problem for inserts with keys redistribution into partitions.
In sample there's a table for 10 years of ordered by some key data that we want to redistribute into partitions by months. IRL there're lots of splits into small parts in partitions on insert that lead to troubles with merges.
```
CREATE TABLE table_src
(
order Int64,
random_date Date
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
ORDER BY order;
CREATE TABLE table_dst
(
order Int64,
random_date Date
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
PARTITION BY toStartOfMonth(random_date)
ORDER BY tuple();
-- 10 years of random dates in source table
INSERT INTO table_src
SELECT number AS order, toDate(rand() % 3650) AS random_date
FROM numbers(1000000);
-- leads to 120 months as partitions in dst table - Too many partitions for single INSERT block
INSERT INTO table_dst
SELECT * FROM table_src; -- { serverError TOO_MANY_PARTS }
-- The same INSERT but groupped by dst partition key & splitted into smaller blocks works well
INSERT INTO table_dst
SELECT * FROM table_src
ORDER BY toStartOfMonth(random_date)
SETTINGS min_insert_block_size_rows = 500000;
```
### Describe the solution you'd like
`INSERT SELECT` should have optional implicit `GROUP BY` by destination table's `PARTITION BY` key. So it could buffer some records in buckets, make insert per bucket logic. So there would be one part/partition for insert and no "Too many partitions for single INSERT block" problem.
1. Set to use N buckets for INSERT
2. When (N+1)'th bucket needed, push the biggest bucket into destination to free one
### Describe alternatives you've considered
It's possible to insert such data avoiding "Too many partitions for single INSERT block" with (optimized) intermediate table sorted by destination's partition key. Or just adding `ORDER BY` with `min_insert_block_size_rows`.
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE table_src (order_id BIGINT, random_date DATE, amount FLOAT) ENGINE = InnoDB;
CREATE TABLE table_dst (order_id BIGINT, random_date DATE, amount FLOAT, INDEX idx_partition (DATE_FORMAT(random_date, '%Y-%m-01'))) ENGINE = InnoDB;

INSERT INTO table_src (order_id, random_date, amount) SELECT number AS order_id, DATE_ADD('2000-01-01', INTERVAL FLOOR(RAND() * 3650) DAY) AS random_date, RAND() * 100 AS amount FROM (SELECT @rownum := @rownum + 1 AS number FROM (SELECT 1 UNION SELECT 2 UNION SELECT 3 UNION SELECT 4 UNION SELECT 5 UNION SELECT 6 UNION SELECT 7 UNION SELECT 8 UNION SELECT 9 UNION SELECT 10) t1, (SELECT @rownum := 0) t2 LIMIT 1000000) AS numbers;
INSERT INTO table_dst (order_id, random_date, amount) SELECT order_id, random_date, amount FROM table_src WHERE amount > 50.0;
INSERT INTO table_dst (order_id, random_date, amount) SELECT order_id, random_date, amount FROM table_src ORDER BY DATE_FORMAT(random_date, '%Y-%m-01') LIMIT 500000;
key_bug_features: `INSERT SELECT` without implicit `GROUP BY` for partition key
